sections.title: Active vs passive
sections.content: **Active:** the subject does the action (*The dog bit the man*). **Passive:** we focus on what happened — the object becomes the subject. We use **be + past participle** (*The man was bitten by the dog*).

sections.title: When to use the passive (1)
sections.content: Use the passive when: the agent is unknown or unimportant (*My bike was stolen*), you want to focus on the action or result (*The report has been finished*), or in formal writing (*The experiment was carried out. It is believed that…*).

sections.title: When to use the passive (2)
sections.content: To say who did it: **by + agent** (*The window was broken by the storm*).

sections.title: Form: be + past participle (1)
sections.content: Passive = **be** (in the right tense) + past participle. The tense goes on **be**, not the participle. **Present:** is/are + pp (*The room is cleaned*). **Past:** was/were + pp (*The room was cleaned*).

sections.title: Form: be + past participle (2)
sections.content: **Present perfect:** has/have been + pp. **Future:** will be + pp. **Modals:** must/can be + pp.

sections.title: By-agent and other prepositions (1)
sections.content: **By** + agent (who did it): *The play was written by Shakespeare.* We often omit **by** when the agent is obvious: *He was arrested. The meeting was postponed.*

sections.title: By-agent and other prepositions (2)
sections.content: Some passives use a different preposition: *interested in* (not by), *filled with*, *known for*.

items.label: Passive:
items.formula: Object → be (tense) + past participle (+ by agent)

items.label: Example:
items.formula: The dog bit the man → The man **was bitten** by the dog

sections.title: Causative: have/get something done (1)
sections.content: We use **have** or **get** + object + past participle when we arrange for someone else to do something, or when something happens to us. *I had my hair cut. She had her car repaired. We got the document translated.*

sections.title: Causative: have/get something done (2)
sections.content: *He had his wallet stolen* (negative experience). **Form:** have/get + object + past participle (not infinitive, not *-ing*).

sections.title: Have vs get
sections.content: **Have** is more neutral and formal. **Get** is more informal and suggests more effort or arrangement: *I got my bike fixed.* Use **have** in formal writing. Both take the same structure: have/get + object + past participle.

sections.title: Typical errors (1)
sections.content: **Wrong:** *The letter was send.* **Right:** **The letter was sent** (past participle). **Wrong:** *The building is built in 1990.* **Right:** **The building was built in 1990** (past time → past passive).

sections.title: Typical errors (2)
sections.content: **Wrong:** *English speaks everywhere.* **Right:** **English is spoken everywhere.** **Wrong:** *I had my hair to cut.* **Right:** **I had my hair cut** (causative: past participle, not infinitive).

sections.title: Reference
sections.content: For more on passive forms and practice, see **Reference**. For causative structures, see the practice sections below.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Passive = was + past participle. Send → sent.

questions.question: Choose the correct passive form.

options.a: English is speak all over the world.
options.b: English speaks all over the world.
options.c: English is spoken all over the world.
options.d: English spoken all over the world.

questions.explanation: Passive: be + past participle. Speak → spoken.

questions.question: 'The building ____ in 1990.' Which fits?
questions.explanation: Past time (1990) → past passive: was/were + past participle.

questions.question: Which is correct with a modal?

options.d: The work must be done by someone.

questions.explanation: Modal + be + past participle. Done is the past participle of do.

questions.question: 'The report ____ yet.' (Present perfect passive)
questions.explanation: Present perfect passive: have/has been + past participle.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct? (causative have/get)

options.a: I had my hair to cut.

questions.explanation: Causative: have + object + past participle. Cut is the past participle.

questions.question: 'She ____ her car ____ last week.' (arranged for someone to repair it)
questions.explanation: Causative: have/get + object + past participle. Had her car repaired.

questions.question: The letter _____ yesterday.
questions.explanation: Past passive: was + past participle (was sent).

questions.question: English _____ in many countries.
questions.explanation: Present simple passive: is + past participle (is spoken).

questions.question: The new hospital _____ last year.
questions.explanation: Past passive: was + past participle (was opened).

questions.question: The work _____ already done.
questions.explanation: Present perfect passive: has been + past participle.

questions.question: The room _____ every day.
questions.explanation: Present simple passive: is + past participle (is cleaned).

questions.question: My bike _____ last week.
questions.explanation: Past passive: was + past participle (was stolen).

questions.question: The report _____ by Friday.
questions.explanation: Modal passive: modal + be + past participle (must be finished).

questions.question: The problem _____ at the meeting tomorrow.
questions.explanation: Future passive: will be + past participle.

questions.question: The play _____ by Shakespeare.
questions.explanation: Past passive: was written (by + agent).

questions.question: These documents _____ on the server.
questions.explanation: Modal passive: can be + past participle (can be found).

questions.question: The letter _____ (send) yesterday.
questions.explanation: Past passive: was + past participle (sent).

questions.question: English _____ (speak) in many countries.
questions.explanation: Present simple passive: is + spoken.

questions.question: The new hospital _____ (open) last year.
questions.explanation: Past passive: was + opened.

questions.question: The work _____ (already / do).
questions.explanation: Present perfect passive: has been + done.

questions.question: The room _____ (clean) every day.
questions.explanation: Present simple passive: is + cleaned.

questions.question: My bike _____ (steal) last week.
questions.explanation: Past passive: was + stolen (steal → stolen).

questions.question: The report _____ (must / finish) by Friday.
questions.explanation: Modal passive: must be + past participle.

questions.question: The play _____ (write) by Shakespeare.
questions.explanation: Past passive: was written. By + agent.

questions.question: The problem _____ (discuss) at the meeting tomorrow.
questions.explanation: Future passive: will be + discussed.

questions.question: This book _____ (read) by millions of people.
questions.explanation: Present perfect or present simple passive: has been read / is read.

questions.question: The window _____ (break) by the storm.
questions.explanation: Past passive: was + broken. By + agent.

questions.question: The documents _____ (can / find) on the server.
questions.explanation: Modal passive: can be + found (find → found).

questions.question: I had my hair _____ (cut) yesterday.
questions.explanation: Causative: have + object + past participle. Cut is the past participle.

questions.question: She had her car _____ (repair) last week.
questions.explanation: Causative: have + object + past participle. Repair → repaired.

questions.question: We got the document _____ (translate) into English.
questions.explanation: Causative: get + object + past participle. Translate → translated.

questions.question: Wrong: 'The letter was send yesterday.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Passive needs past participle: send → sent.

questions.question: Wrong: 'English is speak all over the world.' Correct the sentence.
answers: English is spoken all over the world.
English is spoken all over the world
questions.explanation: Passive: be + past participle (spoken).

questions.question: Wrong: 'The building is built in 1990.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Past time (1990) → past passive: was built.

questions.question: Wrong: 'The work must be done by someone.' (If you mean the work is necessary.) Correct to a natural passive.
questions.explanation: By someone is unnecessary; the passive alone is enough.

questions.question: Wrong: 'The report has not been finish yet.' Correct the sentence.
answers: The report has not been finished yet.
The report has not been finished yet
questions.explanation: Present perfect passive: have been + past participle (finished).

questions.question: Wrong: 'He was arrest by the police.' Correct the sentence.
answers: He was arrested by the police.
He was arrested by the police
questions.explanation: Passive: was + past participle (arrested).

questions.question: Wrong: 'It can be do tomorrow.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Modal passive: can be + past participle (done).

questions.question: Wrong: 'The meeting was postpone.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Passive: was + past participle (postponed).

questions.question: Wrong: 'I had my hair to cut.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Causative: have + object + past participle (not infinitive). Cut is the past participle.

questions.question: Wrong: 'She got her eyes testing.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Causative: get + object + past participle. Test → tested.

questions.question: 'He ____ his wallet ____ on the bus.' (something bad happened to him)
questions.explanation: Causative/experience: have + object + past participle. Had his wallet stolen.
